response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior/staff-level individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators, device orchestration tools) and well over 8 years of development experience. However, this is a Level II people-management role, and the resume shows no formal people management or team leadership experience — every position is an IC role, and leadership signals are limited to community teaching, workshops, and founding a small open-source project. There is also no evidence of organizational change leadership or strategic roadmap ownership at the scale this role requires.
strengths: Deep developer-tools experience: built test runners, assertion libraries, CI servers, and internal device orchestration tooling
Broad technical range across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, including iOS/Apple TV work relevant to a mobile ecosystem
Strong engineering-culture instincts: TDD, trunk-based development, code katas, and active knowledge sharing through workshops and talks
concerns: No formal people management experience — fails the 2-year minimum requirement for this manager role
No demonstrated technical leadership of teams (roadmaps, performance management, org change) beyond individual contribution and community teaching
Mobile experience is dated and app-level rather than platform/build-infrastructure scale implied by the role

key_factors.factor: Hands-on developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
Mentoring and teaching signals without formal leadership scope
